title: Fitting a suspended ceiling tile grid
subtitle: Install a metal grid system suspended on wires from an existing ceiling, then drop acoustic or mineral tiles into the grid
excerpt: How to fit a suspended ceiling tile grid. Covers setting the grid height, fixing perimeter angle, hanging the main tees and cross tees on wire hangers, and dropping 600 × 600 mm tiles into the completed grid.
sourceNotes: UK suspended ceiling installation trade practice. Armstrong World Industries and similar generic suspended ceiling system guides (referenced principles). Black and Decker Complete Photo Guide to Home Improvement (referenced, not reproduced).

glossaryTerms.definition: The primary structural member of a suspended ceiling grid. A T-profile galvanised steel section hung on wires from the structural ceiling above, running the full length of the room at 1200 mm centres. Cross tees slot into the main tees at 600 mm centres to form the 600 × 600 mm grid into which mineral or acoustic tiles drop. Main tees come in 3600 mm lengths and splice together with factory interlocking joints.

content.text: A suspended ceiling grid sits below the existing ceiling on wire hangers and holds 600 × 600 mm mineral tiles. Mark the height on all four walls, fix the perimeter angle strip, hang the
on wires, slot the cross tees between them, and drop the tiles in.
Set the grid height and mark the wall angle

The grid hangs at least 100 mm below the existing ceiling (more if concealing services. Mark the finished grid height on one wall. Transfer the level around all four walls with a spirit level or water level. Snap a chalk line at this height and fix the perimeter wall angle) an L-section galvanised strip (with screws into plugs or directly into timber studs at 400 mm centres. The perimeter angle supports the edges of the border tiles and the ends of the first and last main tees.

Plan the grid so the border tiles are equal on opposite sides and at least half a tile wide. Measure the room length and width; divide by 600 mm and distribute any remainder equally between the two opposite edges. Mark the main tee positions on the wall angles at 1200 mm centres, starting from a central line.

Fix screw eyes into the structural ceiling at 1200 mm centres along each main tee line; plug into concrete or screw into joists. Hang galvanised suspension wire through each eye, coiling the tail back on itself. Rest each main tee on the wall angle at each end and adjust the wire hangers to level the tee. Use the spirit level on each tee before moving to the next.
Slot in the cross tees and drop the tiles

Slot the 600 mm cross tees into the pressed slots in the main tees at 600 mm centres. The cross tee tabs lock with a quarter-turn and a click. Work across the room from one end, dropping full tiles into each completed grid square as you go. For border tiles, cut to size with a utility knife and straight edge) score the face deeply and snap cleanly. Fit the cut edge against the wall.

attrs.symptom: The completed grid shows visible dips and humps when viewed from across the room
attrs.cause: The wire hangers are not all adjusted to the same height, or the perimeter wall angle is not level.
attrs.fix: Re-check the wall angle level on all four walls first. Then re-tension individual hangers on the offending main tees until a straight-edge laid across the grid shows no deviation.
